items.why: The trade deadline panel exists and is browser-visible, but live code only renders advisory cards. The rebrand review specifically calls for deterministic deadline offers generated from standings, needs, and cap/trade discipline instead of hard-coded old-app behavior.
evidence: public/lib/tradeDeadlineFrenzy.js currently returns three copy-only offers per buyer/seller/swing role.
public/lib/tabOverview.js renders the panel only during regular-season weeks 9-11, so the feature has a safe, narrow browser surface.
docs/rebrand/GRIDIRON_GM_VAULT_MERGE_REVIEW.md prioritizes Trade Deadline Frenzy as Wave 2 and warns to avoid fixed pick-cost behavior.
rg found no existing focused test for tradeDeadlineFrenzy.js, so regression coverage is missing.
items.recipe: Upgrade buildTradeDeadlineFrenzy to generate deterministic offer cards with partner, target need, cap impact, asset ask, constraint, and risk from live dashboard inputs; render those fields in the existing panel; add focused tests proving role selection, determinism, and cap/challenge-safe constraints.
shippedEvidence: public/lib/tradeDeadlineFrenzy.js returns structured deterministic deadline offers
test/trade-deadline-frenzy.test.js covers buyer, cap/challenge constraints, and determinism

rejected.reason: Still blocked on real received-message proof for football@playfranchisearchitect.com forwarding/copying and current live-origin/routing evidence.
Already present in Sim-Watch as a current field-position visualization; further work would need fresh play-by-play data design, not a quick port.
